permissions.description: Gives an extension temporary access to the currently active tab when the user invokes the extension as when clicking its browser action. For example, with user interaction, the extension can inject JavaScript. Access to the tab lasts while the user is on that page, and is revoked when the user navigates away or closes the tab.
Integrate menus for extension throughout Chrome browser
The chrome.declarativeNetRequest API is used to block or redirect network requests by specifying declarative rules

content_scripts.description: Grants the extension access to all HTTP URLs, which can pose a significant security risk as it may allow the extension to access sensitive data transmitted over unencrypted connections. This permission should be used with extreme caution and only when absolutely necessary, preferring HTTPS connections whenever possible.

Grants the extension access to all HTTPS URLs, which can potentially expose sensitive user data or compromise user privacy. This permission should be used carefully and with proper justification, limiting access to only the necessary domains or URLs.

web_accessible_resources.description: Grants the extension access to all URLs, regardless of protocol (HTTP or HTTPS), which can pose a significant security risk as it may allow the extension to access sensitive data transmitted over unencrypted connections or compromise user privacy. This permission should be used with extreme caution and only when absolutely necessary, preferring HTTPS connections whenever possible.
